stem: To drive economically in a petrol car you should change up:

choices.text: Only when the engine struggles
At around 2,500 rpm
Above 4,000 rpm
Only at the speed limit

explanation.short: Around 2,500 rpm for petrol (and 2,000 rpm for diesel) is fuel-efficient.
explanation.examTip: Accelerate firmly, change up early — short bursts at high rpm waste fuel.

stem: The most fuel-efficient way to slow down for a red light ahead is to:

choices.text: Brake hard at the last moment
Keep the throttle on until the line, then brake
Lift off early and let the engine brake the car (motorrem)
Put the car in neutral and coast

explanation.short: Lifting off early uses engine braking and cuts the fuel supply.
explanation.examTip: Coasting in neutral is forbidden because you lose engine control of the car.

stem: Compared with driving 120 km/h on the motorway, driving at 100 km/h:

choices.text: Uses about the same amount of fuel
Uses noticeably more fuel
Saves about 10–20% in fuel
Damages the engine

explanation.short: Air resistance grows sharply with speed; 100 vs 120 km/h saves 10–20%.
explanation.examTip: Less speed also means lower emissions and shorter stopping distance.

choices.text: Saves fuel because the contact patch is bigger
Increases rolling resistance and fuel use
Has no effect on fuel use
Increases grip in all conditions

explanation.short: Soft tyres roll harder, raising fuel use and wear.
explanation.examTip: Check pressures cold at least once a month.

stem: You will not use the roof box for the next months. From an eco-driving point of view you should:

choices.text: Leave it on — removing it makes no difference
Remove it, because it raises fuel use significantly at speed
Fill it with something heavy so it sits stable
Move it to the back instead

explanation.short: An unused roof box increases drag and can add up to 20% fuel use on motorways.
explanation.examTip: Extra weight in the car also costs fuel — empty the boot of unnecessary items.

stem: Which maintenance issue typically raises fuel consumption?

choices.text: A clogged air filter
A clean windscreen
New wiper blades
A full washer fluid reservoir

explanation.short: A blocked air filter forces the engine to work harder and burn more fuel.
explanation.examTip: Worn plugs and dirty injectors have the same effect — keep service intervals.

stem: In an electric or hybrid car, the best way to recover energy is to:

choices.text: Brake as hard as possible every time
Anticipate and brake gently and earlier
Coast in neutral as much as possible
Switch the system off in town

explanation.short: Gentle, earlier braking maximises regenerative energy recovery.
explanation.examTip: Very hard braking still wastes energy as heat through the friction brakes.

stem: Which factor most reduces the range of an electric car?

choices.text: Driving slowly in town
Cold weather combined with motorway speeds and heating
Using the radio
Carrying one passenger

explanation.short: Cold weather, high speed and cabin heating together drop EV range sharply.
explanation.examTip: Pre-condition the cabin while still plugged in to save driving range.

stem: A public charging bay is reserved for:

choices.text: Any electric car, charging or not
Electric and hybrid cars that are actually charging
Residents of the street only
Disabled drivers

explanation.short: Charging bays are only for vehicles that are connected and charging.
explanation.examTip: Move the car promptly when charging is finished.

stem: You expect to wait more than 30 seconds at a closed level crossing. The most eco-friendly action is to:

choices.text: Keep the engine running so the car is ready
Switch off the engine while waiting
Rev the engine to warm it up
Reverse a few metres to ease pressure on the brakes

explanation.short: Idling longer than about 30 seconds uses more fuel than restarting.
explanation.examTip: Many modern cars have stop-start that does this automatically.

stem: Cyclists are blocking the narrow street ahead and you cannot easily pass. The responsible response is to:

choices.text: Sound the horn until they move aside
Drive close behind to encourage them
Stay back at a calm pace and wait for a safe moment
Overtake on the pavement

explanation.short: Accept losing a few seconds rather than forcing a pass that endangers cyclists.
explanation.examTip: Calm driving in town is safer, quieter and cleaner.

stem: An environmental zone (milieuzone) in a Dutch city mainly aims to:

choices.text: Keep tourists out of the centre
Reduce emissions by excluding the most polluting vehicles
Slow all traffic to 15 km/h
Provide free parking

explanation.short: Milieuzones exclude older, more polluting vehicles to improve city air.
explanation.examTip: Each city sets its own exact rules — check signs and city websites.

stem: On a flat road in a manual car, what is the most fuel-efficient general rule?

choices.text: Stay in low gears at high revs
Shift up early and drive in the highest practical gear
Always use the lowest gear available
Keep the engine speed above 4000 rpm

explanation.short: High gear, low revs reduce fuel use and emissions on flat, steady stretches.
explanation.examTip: Aim to shift up around 2000–2500 rpm in petrol cars and even earlier in diesels.

stem: Approaching a red traffic light you can already see ahead, the most eco-friendly action is to:

choices.text: Hold full speed and brake hard at the line
Release the accelerator early and roll up in gear so the engine cuts injection
Shift to neutral and coast
Accelerate hoping the light changes

explanation.short: Lifting off in gear lets modern cars cut fuel injection; you save fuel while slowing down naturally.
explanation.examTip: Coasting in neutral is less efficient and reduces engine braking control.

stem: Tyres that are under-inflated have what main effect on the car?

choices.text: Lower fuel consumption
Higher rolling resistance and higher fuel consumption
Better grip in all weather
Longer tyre life

explanation.short: Under-inflated tyres raise rolling resistance, fuel use and wear, and harm handling.
explanation.examTip: Check pressures monthly cold, and increase pressure when carrying heavy loads.

stem: You see a sign for a 'milieuzone' (environmental zone) in a Dutch city. What does it mean?

choices.text: Free parking for all cars
Only vehicles meeting the local emission rules may enter
Cyclists must dismount
The road is closed at weekends

explanation.short: Environmental zones bar older, polluting vehicles; the exact rules depend on the city and vehicle category.
explanation.examTip: Check the sign carefully — diesel cars and trucks usually face stricter rules than petrol cars.
